name: forktex-py
description: ForkTex Python repo — the CLI, agent, architecture discovery, FSD audit, and orchestration toolkit (single-package repo)

typing.description: Static type system verifies interface correctness (pyright; install via dev deps)

security.description: Scan installed Python deps for known CVEs (pip-audit; install via dev deps)

build.description: Build sdist + wheel into dist/ and verify metadata with twine check

publish-test.description: Upload dist/ to TestPyPI (https://test.pypi.org) for rehearsal. SDK deps must already be on TestPyPI.

publish.description: Upload dist/ to production PyPI (irreversible per-version)

acceptance.description: Install the published wheel into a fresh venv and battle-test the CLI end-to-end (forktex --version, every subcommand --help, fsd check + graph build against forktex-py itself)
commands: @if ! ls dist/forktex-*.whl >/dev/null 2>&1; then echo '  building wheel first...'; $(MAKE) build; fi
@command -v python3.14 >/dev/null 2>&1 || { echo '  ✗ no python3.14 on PATH (the published wheel pins Python ≥ 3.14)' >&2; exit 1; }
@for sub in agents clean cloud fsd graph intelligence manual network serve status; do /tmp/forktex-accept-venv/bin/forktex $$sub --help > /dev/null && echo "  ✓ forktex $$sub --help"; done
@echo '' && echo 'acceptance: forktex CLI installs + invokes correctly'

dev-link-sdks.description: pip install -e the three sibling SDK repos (cloud/intelligence/network) for local development against source trees
commands: @bash -c 'for d in ../cloud/sdk-py ../intelligence/sdk-py ../network/sdk-py; do [ -d "$$d" ] || { echo "missing: $$d"; exit 1; }; done'
@echo 'linked. export FORKTEX_DEV_SIBLING_SDKS=1 to flag the dev-linked banner on forktex --version'

dev-unlink-sdks.description: Reinstall the three SDKs from their pinned PyPI versions, restoring the published surface

dev-install.description: One-shot editable install of the four ecosystem packages (cloud, intelligence, network SDKs + forktex-py CLI) from sibling source trees
commands: @bash -c 'for d in ../cloud/sdk-py ../intelligence/sdk-py ../network/sdk-py; do [ -d "$$d" ] || { echo "missing: $$d"; exit 1; }; done'
@echo 'installed. run "export FORKTEX_DEV_SIBLING_SDKS=1" to flag the dev-linked banner on forktex --version.'

installer-build.description: Bundle scripts/install.sh + install.ps1 with _install_core.py inlined into dist/install/ for hosting

installer-test.description: Run the installer across Linux distros via Docker (ubuntu/fedora/arch). Requires installer-build first.

license-check.description: Verify every source file carries the current AGPL-3.0 + Commercial dual-license header (CI gate)

license-fix.description: Add or update the dual-license header on every source file (idempotent)

license-strip.description: Remove the dual-license header from every source file (use before license model changes)

sync.description: Not applicable: forktex-py has no schema-derived artifacts (pure Python CLI, no generated code)
commands: @echo 'sync: not applicable for forktex-py (no codegen artifacts to keep current)'

gate.description: Pre-merge quality gate: format-check + lint + license-check + security + test + build (renamed from `ci`)
commands: @echo 'Gate passed for $(PROJECT_NAME) — safe to: make publish-test  /  make publish'
@echo '(make typing reports cross-SDK type drifts; tracked separately)'

manual.description: Build the system-wide architecture + AI context manual from the project graph (humans + agents).

packages.name: forktex
packages.description: CLI, agent, MCP-style orchestrator, architecture discovery, and audit toolkit for ForkTex
